rationale: The citing paper does more than list this source in background; it uses Danaher et al. (2008) for a specific empirical claim that competitive advertising clutter reduces advertising elasticity when interpreting its own time-trend findings. It also cites Danaher et al. for a concrete methodological step in the meta-analysis: converting ad-stock-based long-term elasticity to short-term elasticity. These are functional uses in interpretation and data harmonization, not a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing meta-analysis draws on the cited paper as empirical support for the mechanism that advertising clutter lowers elasticity, which helps explain observed declines over time. It also relies on the cited paper's elasticity conversion expression when standardizing effect sizes across studies.
